retrieved.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
retrieved.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

heading_path: EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

reranked.title: Web services
reranked.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.

2. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. reranked.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.

You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

reranked.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

selected.title: Web services
selected.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps
